Recurring Invoices

Recurring invoices are templates that automatically generate sales invoices on a regular schedule. You set up the client, line items, frequency, and billing day once, and Merlin creates the actual invoice at the right time — and optionally emails it to the client automatically. This is useful for subscriptions, retainers, and any service billed on a fixed periodic basis.

Overview

Go to Billing › Recurring Invoices to see all recurring invoice schedules. Each schedule shows the client, frequency, next invoice date, and status. Statuses are active (will generate invoices on schedule), paused (temporarily stopped), and completed (the end date has passed or the schedule has been manually completed).

Creating a recurring invoice

  1. Go to Billing › Recurring Invoices.
  2. Click New Recurring Invoice.
  3. Select the client and fill in the schedule fields.
  4. Add line items (description, quantity, unit price, tax rate).
  5. Save.

Key fields when creating a recurring invoice:

FieldNotes
ClientThe client to invoice
Payment termUsed to calculate the due date on each generated invoice
Receivable accountGL account for the income on each generated invoice
Contact(s)Which contact people receive the invoice email (when auto-send is on)
FrequencyHow often to generate: monthly, quarterly, or annually
Billing period dayDay of the month on which the invoice is generated
Start dateDate from which the schedule is active
End dateOptional date after which no more invoices are generated
Next invoice dateThe date of the next invoice to be generated; updated automatically after each generation
CurrencyCurrency for the generated invoices
Auto-sendWhether to email the invoice to the client automatically on generation
Notes / Terms / FooterOptional text copied onto each generated invoice

Frequency and billing day

The frequency field controls the interval between invoices:

  • Monthly — an invoice is generated each month on the billing period day
  • Quarterly — an invoice is generated every three months
  • Annually — an invoice is generated once a year

The billing_period_day field sets the day of the month on which each invoice is generated. For example, a billing period day of 1 generates invoices on the 1st of each month (for monthly frequency).

Auto-send

When auto_send is enabled on a recurring invoice, each generated sales invoice is automatically emailed to the selected contacts using the NettMail template configured in Settings › Billing. This happens in the same step as generation — no manual send action is required. If auto_send is off, the generated invoice appears in the Sales Invoices list in draft status, ready for you to review and send manually.

How invoices are generated

The GenerateRecurringInvoices Artisan command runs automatically every day at 06:00, scheduled via Laravel's task scheduler. It checks all active recurring invoice schedules whose next_invoice_date is today or earlier, generates a Document record for each one, and advances the schedule's next_invoice_date to the next due date. If auto_send is enabled, the invoice email is dispatched immediately after generation.

Permissions

  • recurring-invoices-view-any / recurring-invoices-view — to view recurring invoice schedules
  • recurring-invoices-create — to create new schedules
  • recurring-invoices-update — to edit existing schedules
  • recurring-invoices-delete — to delete schedules
  • recurring-invoices-restore / recurring-invoices-force-delete — to restore or permanently delete soft-deleted schedules
